title: andropogon sorghum var. usorum
intro: Andropogon sorghum var. usorum, commonly known as broom sorghum, is a species of plant in the grass family that is native to Africa and parts of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The health benefits of broom sorghum include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting digestive health. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or powder, and is also used in the production of natural remedies such as salves and ointments for skin care and wound healing.
